shortDescription.text: Insecure Direct Object Reference via predictable or guessable token
Rate limiter bypass via IP address spoofing through client-controlled headers
Unauthorized workflow continuation by unauthenticated form submission

message.text: The POST /api/form/human_input/<form_token> endpoint is intentionally unauthenticated (comment: 'this endpoint is unauthenticated on purpose for now'). The commented-out _end_user parameter means submission_end_user_id is always None, so there is no identity binding between the form and the submitter. Any party who obtains or guesses a form_token can submit the form and trigger workflow continuation (resume_app_execution). This defeats authorization controls on workflow state transitions.

The rate limiter keys on the client IP obtained via extract_remote_ip(), which trusts the X-Forwarded-For and CF-Connecting-IP headers without validation. An attacker can rotate these header values to bypass the rate limit entirely, enabling brute-force enumeration of form tokens or repeated submission attempts. The rate limiter is the only protective control on this unauthenticated endpoint.

Form tokens are the sole authorization gate for both GET (reading form definitions including rendered content) and POST (submitting input and resuming workflows). If tokens are insufficiently random (e.g., UUIDs without cryptographic randomness) or leaked via logs/referrer headers, any unauthenticated user can access or submit arbitrary forms. The service layer (get_form_by_token) performs no caller identity check beyond token possession.

A successful POST submission by an unauthenticated attacker calls submit_form_by_token which invokes enqueue_resume → resume_app_execution.apply_async, continuing a paused workflow. Because submission_end_user_id is hardcoded to None, there is no audit trail of who actually submitted the form. An attacker with a valid form_token can approve or reject workflow steps, potentially authorizing actions (e.g., financial approvals, deployments) without legitimate user consent.
